name: Gwyn ap Nudd
description: Gwyn ap Nudd is a prominent figure in Welsh mythology, often described as the king of the Tylwyth Teg, the Welsh fairy folk, and ruler of Annwn, the Otherworld. He is a complex character, simultaneously portrayed as a fearsome warrior, a skilled hunter, and a psychopomp, guiding souls to the afterlife. Gwyn is frequently associated with the Wild Hunt, leading a spectral pack of hounds across the sky, pursuing the souls of the dead. He is also known for his abduction of Creiddylad, daughter of Lludd Llaw Eraint, which led to a perpetual battle with Gwythyr ap Greidawl for her hand, fought annually on May Day until the end of time, as prophesied by Arthur. Gwyn's power extends over the animal kingdom, particularly dogs and birds, and he possesses knowledge of hidden pathways and secrets. He is a figure of both dread and respect, embodying the untamed forces of nature and the mysteries of the Otherworld. His role in Welsh folklore highlights the liminal spaces between the mortal and supernatural realms, and the enduring power of ancient traditions.
subtitle: King of the Welsh Otherworld

keyMyths.title: The Abduction of Creiddylad
keyMyths.description: Gwyn ap Nudd abducts Creiddylad, leading to an eternal battle with Gwythyr ap Greidawl for her hand. Arthur intervenes and decrees they fight annually until Judgment Day. This myth highlights themes of rivalry, fate, and Arthurian intervention.

keyMyths.title: Leader of the Wild Hunt
keyMyths.description: Gwyn leads the Wild Hunt, a spectral procession of hounds and spirits, across the sky. This hunt is often associated with death and the transition of souls to the Otherworld, solidifying Gwyn's role as a psychopomp. The hunt is a terrifying spectacle, a reminder of the power of the supernatural.

keyMyths.title: King of Annwn
keyMyths.description: Gwyn reigns as king of Annwn, the Welsh Otherworld, a realm of mystery and magic. He commands the Tylwyth Teg and holds sway over its inhabitants. This position grants him immense power and knowledge of the hidden realms.

sources.author: Unknown
